title: NumPy Array
documentation:
## Purpose
NumPy's native array format stores N-dimensional arrays with a small header describing dtype, shape, and byte order followed by raw array data. The [NumPy Array](https://capdag.com/browse/media:numpy) media type covers both `.npy` (single array) and `.npz` (zip archive of multiple named arrays) files.

## When to use
Use this type when a node produces or consumes numerical arrays intended for Python-based downstream processing — for example, intermediate feature matrices, model inputs/outputs, or pre-computed embeddings. Prefer HDF5 for hierarchical or very large datasets; use NumPy format when simplicity and direct `np.load` interoperability matter most.

## Notes
`.npy` files begin with a magic string `\x93NUMPY` followed by version bytes and a Python-literal header dict. `.npz` archives are standard ZIP files and can be read without NumPy using any ZIP library. Implementers should use `numpy.save` / `numpy.load` for single arrays and `numpy.savez` / `numpy.savez_compressed` for multi-array bundles.
